name: Logarithmic Intensity Rescaling of Eagle Nebula H-alpha Image
description: An astrophotographer needs to compress the enormous dynamic range of a deep H-alpha observation of the Eagle Nebula to make both the bright pillars and faint extended emission visible simultaneously. Using the raw FITS image at ~/AstroImages/eagle_log/eagle_halpha.fits, apply a log(1+x) transformation to the pixel values and rescale the resulting image by a factor of 10000. Save the transformed image to ~/AstroImages/eagle_log/output/eagle_halpha_logscaled.fits. Document the statistical changes in a plain text report at ~/AstroImages/eagle_log/output/transform_report.txt using the following keys (one per line, format 'KEYWORD: value'): ORIGINAL_MIN, ORIGINAL_MAX, ORIGINAL_MEAN, ORIGINAL_STDDEV, TRANSFORMED_MIN, TRANSFORMED_MAX, TRANSFORMED_MEAN, TRANSFORMED_STDDEV, DYNAMIC_RANGE_ORIGINAL (calculated as max / mean), DYNAMIC_RANGE_TRANSFORMED (calculated as max / mean), and TRANSFORM_METHOD (which must be 'log1p').
